  • OCR for quick data capture Link

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

  • Automatic creation of maintenance notifications for condition-enabled measuring points Link
  • Enable in-house refurbishment Link
  • OCR for quick data capture Link

SAP Maintenance Assistant mobile app

  • AI-enabled maintenance and service execution Link
  • Support for Esri maps integration Link
  • Supporting SAP Dynamic Forms Link

 

Metering

  • Graphical display of utilities interval data Link
  • More flexible status handling for energy data management formulas Link
  • Archiving of technical master data that is specific to the utilities industry Link
  • Enabling custom fields and logic for point-of-delivery and BPEM case data Link

Analytics

  • Extraction of Energy data management related master data Link
  • Using SAP Datasphere in the utilities industry – utilities sales contracts Link
  • Native sales statistics and unbilled revenue reporting in SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud Link

Convergent Invoicing

  • End-to-end reconciliation from event to GL in contract accounting and invoicing Link
  • Increase automation of invoicing processes by leveraging machine learning and predictive models Link
  • Reduce time and costs associated with simulation of invoice forecasts for subscription or usage alterations Link

Financials (FI-CA)

  • Display of utilities invoices Link and budget billing plans Link in a business partner financial overview
  • Budget billing plans in the Business Partner Financial Overview app Link
  • Display of utilities invoices in a business partner financial overview Link

 

Customer and Service Management

  • Generative AI–driven digital agent for customer self-service and customer engagement Link
  • Service management for utilities – round-off Link
  • Period control for life cycle events Link
  • Streamlining contract migration and bulk data processing Link
  • Extending pricing capabilities Link
  • Digital payments for service transactions  Link
  • Incorporating the Joule assistant Link

Sales for Commodity & Non-commodity

  • Alignment with the product model of SAP S/4HANA Link
  • Product replication using SAP Master Data Integration Link
  • Intelligent sales execution – target planning Link
  • Intelligent sales execution – product- and volume-based pipeline management Link
  • AI product configuration Link
  • New user interface for the document generation process on the Quote Link
  • Usage-based pricing with integration with SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management Link

Commerce Telco & Utilities Accelerator

  • Streamlined material replication for smoother integration Link

Marketing

  • Personalization and segmentation accelerator for utilities Link
  • Advanced personalization and segmentation accelerator for utilities Link

CX AI Toolkit

  • Shopping assistant agent: personalized recommendations Link

 

Distributed Energy Resources (DER)

  • Cancellation of measurement concept instances before device installation Link
  • Analytics for Long-Running Processes Link
  • Enabling validation checks of instances through an API Link
  • Export of a network graph for usage in third-party systems Link
  • Advanced asset health monitoring scenarios with embedded IoT Link
  • AI-powered Joule digital assistant for navigational and transactional use cases Link
  • Integrating AI-based electric vehicle supply equipment (EVSE) asset management Link
  • E-Mobility integration to complementary ecosystem solutions such as energy management Link

Circular Economy

  • Generate a generic waste transportation document to enable shipment of waste Link
  • Integration of waste management and purchasing processes Link
  • Initial Release of SAP Spend Control Tower to provide an enterprise view of total spend Link

Climate Action

  • Carbon emissions allowance management Link
  • Environment management for energy consumption data Link
  • Carbon border adjustment mechanism (CBAM): content with an operator focus Link

Sustainability Accounting

  • Collection and allocation of greenhouse gas emissions in a carbon journal Link
  • Importing emission quantities through file upload Link
  • Accounting for carbon allowances in a carbon journal Link
  • Enabling automatic carbon collections and automated allocations Link
  • Posting and analysis of carbon quantities on external suppliers / affiliated companies Link

E-mobility

  • Charging cost reimbursement for employee charging Link
  • Enabling import and processing of charging sessions from external charge point management systems Link
  • Smart Charging configuration based on user defined parameters Link
  • CO2-based electric vehicle (EV) charging and pricing tariffs handling Link
  • Compatibility with ISO 15118 to support plug-and-charge functionality Link
  • EV Driver authorizations and invoicing in charging groups Link
  • Unidirectional smart charging (V1G) and bi-directional smart charging (V2G) Link

Social Responsibility

  • Electronic submission of recordable injury or illness cases to OSHA as per the legal requirements (cloud) Link
  • Operational risk management: approval of safety instructions for chemicals and equipment (cloud) Link
  • Support for standard, analytical content for workplace safety and social responsibility (cloud) Link
  • Operational risk assessment: visualization of risk history Link

Dangerous Goods Management and Recycling Administration

  • Storing attributes of batteries as basis for the dangerous goods classification Link
  • Additional regulation-specific details for dangerous goods regulations Link
  • Defining customer-specific dangerous goods description templates Link
  • Support for USG-04 in IATA Dangerous Goods Regulations Link

Holistic Steering and Reporting

  • Narrative disclosures, ESG-metric definition, and data collection Link
  • Custom metrics with specific dimension breakdown Link

Please consider that this information and SAP‘s strategy and possible future developments are subject to change and may be changed by SAP at any time for any reason without notice. This document is provided without a warranty of any kind, either express or implied, including but not limited to, the implied warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, or non-infringement.

entry.title: SAP Asset Performance Management in Maritime Industry
entry.content:
As a Marine Engineer with years of experience working on ships, I've seen firsthand the challenges of maintaining complex machinery in harsh maritime environments. One solution that stands out for its potential to revolutionize ship maintenance is SAP Asset Performance Management (APM). By leveraging SAP APM, we can significantly enhance the efficiency of our machines and reduce maintenance costs.

A ship presents an excellent use case for SAP APM due to its repetitive operations and the concept of sister ships. Typically, sister ships have identical machinery make and model, allowing for the creation of a standardized template for onboard machinery. This template can be directly applied to sister ships and, with minor adjustments, across the entire fleet. Such a template enhances efficiency and significantly reduces the time required to implement SAP APM and S4 Enterprise Asset Management (EAM).

The foundational structure can be established in S4, with initial strategies derived from OEM instruction manuals or the Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) of the owners or management company, based on their extensive experience with the machinery. This approach also facilitates learning from issues encountered on one ship, thereby preventing similar problems on other ships.

Modern ships are outfitted with numerous sensors and data points, and regular logbook entries can be directly input into S4. This combination of sensor data and manual entries allows for the easy maintenance of time series data, which can be synced with the Internet of Things (IoT) to generate trends and predict failures or anomalous behavior. Connectivity at sea is no longer an issue, thanks to VSAT and Starlink, which provide global internet coverage, even in open seas and international waters.

SAP APM is a leading cloud-based SaaS product designed to monitor the overall performance of machinery. It falls under the IAM/EAM umbrella and is applicable across any industry where machinery maintenance is essential. SAP APM seamlessly integrates with S4 EAM (both cloud and on-premise) for planning and scheduling, and with mobility solutions like SAP Service and Asset Management (SSAM) for execution and feedback.

SAP APM is a unique solution which brings together multiple important functions required for an effective and mature maintenance management. It can be broadly divided into two functions-

  1. Asset Strategy
  2. Asset Health

These components of SAP APM can further be divided into different applications and processes as mentioned below-

1. Asset Strategy:

    1. R&C- Risk and Criticality
    2. SAC- Strategy Assessment for Class
    3. RCM- Reliability Centered Maintenance
    4. FMEA- Failure Mode and Effect Analysis
    5. Recommendation

2. Asset Health:

    1. Inspections
    2. Indicator Monitoring
    3. Rules and Alerts
    4. Failure Curve Analytics
    5. IoT

Asset strategies can easily be defined and reused across the ships in the fleet as the operating conditions and parameters will be very similar in similar types of ships. The comparisons can then be made across ships to finetune the strategy by learning from each other’s data and observations.

Reliability engineers can optimize and align maintenance strategies by carefully evaluating operating conditions and machinery types based on their criticality. They can recommend proactive, reactive, and improvement tasks for equipment with similar characteristics. These recommendations can then be converted into task lists and maintenance plans, generating maintenance orders to execute the tasks. Continuous feedback and monitoring help adjust these recommendations, potentially saving millions of dollars by avoiding unnecessary maintenance costs in spare parts, maintenance crew, and downtime required for routine maintenance tasks.

Asset health can be monitored by evaluating indicator values from S4 measurement documents or sensors. These readings can be synced with IoT, allowing time series data to be analyzed for machine performance and failure prediction. This proactive approach helps prevent machinery breakdowns and plant shutdowns, potentially saving millions of dollars in maintenance costs, unplanned material, and service procurement. 

SAP APM offers several benefits for the maritime industry:

  • Risk & Criticality Assessment: This feature helps identify critical equipment, allowing for decisions on whether to implement a rigorous maintenance schedule or run the machinery to failure.
  • Trend Analysis: The vast amount of data generated from multiple ships can enhance machine learning models, leading to more accurate failure predictions.
  • Asset Health Monitoring: By establishing rules to track parameters such as vibration, temperature, and pressure, alerts can be generated when these parameters exceed defined limits.
  • Strategy Assessment: Developing strategies for equipment classes can significantly reduce maintenance costs by avoiding unnecessary repetitive maintenance.
  • Failure Curve Analysis: This tool predicts the remaining useful life of equipment, enabling informed decisions on when to perform maintenance.

 

Stay tuned! In my upcoming blogs, I'll delve into specific use cases for SAP APM.

I’ll update this blog with links to the future posts.

 

Useful links:

IAM- Intelligent Asset Management: This is a suite of cloud and on-premise solutions such as SAP APM and S4 EAM focused on the maintenance of machinery in simple terms.

SAP Asset Performance Management (APM): This is a cloud product from SAP to help reliability engineers.

S4 EAM- Enterprise Asset Management: This is SAP’s solution for maintenance initiation, planning, scheduling, execution and closure integrated with materials management and finance.

IoT- Internet of Things: IoT is a network which collects data from physical devices as a time series for analysis and tracking of trends to predict behavior.

 

FAQs:

1. Is SAP APM a cloud or on-premise solution?

  • SAP APM is a cloud product which regularly receives new updates and features. It is true cloud product which is continuously evolving and improving based on customer feedback and SAP’s innovation.

2. Can SAP APM be implemented as a stand-alone system?

  • It cannot be implemented as a stand-alone system. It needs a backend S$ EAM system to replicate and sync the master data.

3. Can SAP APM be integrated with S4 EAM’s cloud version or on-premise version?

  • It can be integrated with both cloud and on-premise versions with certain pre-requisites.

4. Can SAP APM be integrated with SAP ECC?

  • There are certain minimum requirements with which SAP APM can be integrated with SAP ERP as well. SAP Mobile Add-On is a prerequisite for the integration of SAP Asset Performance Management with the SAP ERP system. The minimum requirement for using the latest SAP Asset Performance Management integration features are as follows:
    • Release Version: SAP ERP: Mobile Add-On for ERP 6.3 SP11
    • Minimum Requirement: SAP ERP: ECC 6 EHP7 SP14

You can find additional details on this link- https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_APM/d70aaf7b5a2543fea6b7ffd952500e78/45db992d0cf544dd97d11616753e30a9.html

entry.title: Integrated Container Tracking Using SAP Enterprise Asset Management and Transportation Management
entry.content:
In the transportation industry, it is common for companies to own and operate transportation units, such as ocean containers, and to require system support to track both their location and condition. While these containers represent a single physical object in the real world, SAP treats them as multiple objects, primarily the Serialized Asset or Equipment in SAP Enterprise Asset Management (EAM) and the Resource in SAP Transportation Management (TM). This dual representation introduces complexity in ensuring synchronization across business processes.

Understanding SAP EAM and SAP TM

Both part of SAP S4/HANA, Enterprise Asset Management is designed to manage the complete lifecycle of physical assets within a company. It includes features for procurement, maintenance, performance monitoring, and limited tracking capabilities. Conversely, SAP Transportation Management focuses on transportation planning, load optimization, and communication with external subcontractors, such as ocean liners. Simplified, EAM is most applicable when containers are within the company’s premises, whereas TM is utilized when containers are in transit.

Many customers request functionality that spans both EAM and TM, necessitating the integration of these objects in SAP. While the optimal solution varies based on specific customer requirements, this blog illustrates how such integration can be achieved.

Integration Example: Tracking Containers in Stock and in Transit

In this example, the customer requires visibility into container locations both in stock and during transit, therefore the container need to be administratively added to the shipment. This is achieved by packing the materials into the container using Handling Unit Management functionality where the packing material (the container) is added as a line item on the Delivery:

Since the container is a serialized material, a serial number must also be assigned. Using the BIC code as the serial number, would be a consideration in this example.

When TM delivery based integration is active, the Freight Unit Building Rule (configuration explained later in this blog) automatically creates a Container Unit document in TM. This links the serialized container (asset / equipment) in the delivery to the TM Container Unit document, as illustrated below:

  • Top Line: The TM Container Unit document, which supports planning and execution in TM and can be used for freight bookings and communication with ocean carriers.
  • Middle Line: The Handling Unit representing the container during packing in the delivery. This establishes the connection between the Equipment/Serialized Material.
  • Bottom Line: The materials that need to be shipped.

When a Goods Issue is posted for the delivery, the container is included and leaves the issuing storage location. Upon posting Goods Receipt at the receiving storage location, the serialized container is added to the stock of the receiving location. This ensures the connection between SAP asset or equipment and the physical container via the Handling Unit, fulfilling the on-premise tracking requirements.

However, it is worth noting that the serial number assigned to the container in the delivery is not passed on to the corresponding container number in the TM Container Unit document. This limitation is inherent to the system and may require a small additional development.

Key Customization Settings

The following customization settings are critical for this integration:

1. Delivery Type Customization: Specific settings are configured in delivery type customization to include containers as packing materials in the delivery.

2. Freight Unit Building Rule: In this set up each container type has its own Freight Unit Building Rule, ensuring the correct corresponding Container Unit document is created based on predefined conditions.

In this example, the Container unit document is created directly in the FUBR, which would be most suitable for FCL scenarios. If consolidation by Freight units would be required, the same could be achieved with the example above, however manual steps would be required to achieve the same packing constellation in the delivery as well as in TM.

entry.title: Achieving Sustainability With SAP Asset Performance Management By Leveraging Industrial Systems.
entry.content:
This blog focuses on the process of setting up Industrial Systems in SAP APM (Asset Performance Management). As part of SAP APM 2411 release, SAP introduced a new application named Industrial Systems to structure and track individual system elements within larger system setups.

The Industrial System is a master data object in SAP Asset Performance Management and is based on industry standard IEC/ISO-81346 for systems that describe several aspects of a technical system. An Industrial System is a set of inter-related objects considered in a defined context as a whole and enables functional, product-related, and location-related views of a technical system.

What is IEC/ISO 81346 Standard:

IEC 81346-1 establishes general principles for the structuring of systems including structuring of information about systems. It also provides rules and guidance for the formulation of unambiguous reference designations for objects in any systems. 

IEC 81346-2 establishes classification schemes with defined object classes and their associated letter codes, and primarily intended for use in reference designations. Designators give an unambiguous way of identifying the aspect element in the system.

Fact Sheet:

IEC International Standards provide the technical foundation that allows countries to increase national innovation and economic productivity as well as upgrade and retrofit cleaner and environmentally sound technologies and industrial processes.

The IEC 81346 standards contribute to Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), including  SDG 8 (Decent Work and Economic Growth), 9 (Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure) & 11 (Sustainable Cities and Communities).

    

In essence,  these standards foster innovation and promote sustainable industrialization by building resilient infrastructure.

Before we dive in to understand industrial systems in SAP Asset Performance Management, we must first understand LEGO bricks. So, here we go.

LEGO Technic NEOM McLaren Formula E Race Car 42169

Building the LEGO Technic NEOM McLaren Formula E Race Car 42169System within the System...

The crux of the Industrial Systems is, the component systems create technical systems and those technical systems create main systems.

Let us dive in...

First things first, enable the Industrial Systems Configuration toggle for Industrial Systems in the Configuration Manager Application and created a reference designator library.

Industry Domain describes the area in which a particular industrial system operates. For instance, I have created MFG - Manufacturing & PRO - Process Industry in addition to the pre-shipped industry domains. Industry domains are a mandatory part of reference designator libraries, which are structured according to IEC/ISO 81346.

Reference Designator Source is the classification standard for the reference designator. 

Aspect is the way of viewing a system in a specified way. A system can contain more than one aspect, with each aspect describing different views of the same system.

We can create any aspect, but it must be assigned to one of the pre-shipped standard aspect types.

A relationship type is defined as the type of relationship between system elements. Relationship type holds the metadata for a relationship, such as source and target entity types, applicable aspect types (Function, Product, Location).

Note: Creation of new relationship types is not supported with the current version of Industrial Systems.

After completing the "Configuration Manager" set up, let us proceed to create "Reference Designator Libraries".

Reference designators are identifiers based on an industry standard library. A Reference Designator Library in SAP Asset Performance Management contains multiple Reference Designator Object Classes.

Note:

  • Schema Category "Aspect Oriented" can only be applied to system elements  of a selected "Aspect Type".
  • Schema Category "Object Oriented" can be applied to all system elements  independent of the "Aspect Type". -  For instance, I have chosen "Object Oriented".

Once the data is updated, we can change the status to "Released" for further processing. The status "Reopened" can be used to edit after the released status.

Now, Let us create the "Industrial System".

Here is my business use case:

I have chosen the "Cooling Water Circulation system" , which is crucial in the Calcination Process Industry. To define hierarchical structure for the "Cooling Tower System" in my calcination plant as per IEC 81346, I have classified the system based on Function, Product and Location aspects.

  • Functional Aspect: Heat Dissipation, Water Circulation, Drive Function and Control & Regulation.
  • Product Aspect: Heat Dissipation System, Pump System1, Pump System2 & Pump System3.
  • Location Aspect: Power Control Center, Motor Control Center, EX-01 Panel , EX-02 Panel & EX-03 Panel, Cooling Tower, Pump House-1.

Also, for instance, I have considered the industrial system perspective "Plant" to represent cooling water circulation system, i.e. Cooling Tower.

  • Equipment: If we want to assign or link an equipment from SAP ERP system to Industrial System.
  • Functional Location: If we want to assign or link an equipment from SAP ERP system to Industrial System.
  • Plant: If we want to create an asset structure for the production plant.

The prefix signs used to indicate the type of "Aspect" in a reference designator code, i.e.

  • = When relating to the function aspect of an object.
  • - When relating to the product aspect of the object.
  • + When relating to the location aspect of the object.

Function Aspect:

Product Aspect:

Location Aspect:

Adding a Product Individual to a Product Aspect. Product individual represents a technical object (equipment or functional location) present in SAP ERP, which instantiates a system element of product aspect type.

Adding a relationship between the system elements and another node.

Mini Note: The connecting arrows only point in the direction of the primary relationships, i.e. Child Of, Realized By, Located at, and Instantiated By.

We can view the basic details of the system element.

This brings me to the end of my process walk-through. I hope my blog proves useful to reliability engineering team, and I look forward seeing them use SAP APM Industrial System application to foster sustainable maintenance operations.

--

Yours Sincerely,

Pardhasaradhi Reddy. Chelikam


 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

My LinkedIn Profile: www.linkedin.com/in/pardhreddyc

entry.title: What is SAP Asset Performance Management (APM)?
entry.content:
SAP Asset Performance Management (APM) is a SaaS solution from SAP which is meant to be a turnkey solution to enhance the S4 HANA Asset Management solution, previously known as SAP Plant Maintenance.

With SAP APM existing customers can transition from the traditional Reactive, Proactive, Improvement maintenance to the upcoming Predictive, Prescriptive and Reliability Centered maintenance.

Being a cloud solution, SAP APM gets regular updates. These updates bring improvements to the existing functionality as well as new functionalities as they are released. Major releases are every 3 months, whereas minor releases could be as frequent as every month. You could also check the link What's New in SAP Asset Performance Management to know more about the released functionalities.

SAP APM is a cloud solution, but it can integrate both with SAP Cloud and On-Premise solutions for Asset Management. It has a subscription model, and the pricing depends on the usage. SAP APM has embedded IoT to store time series data, and it can also be used for device connectivity. SAP APM also comes with a lot of public APIs which could be very useful to query the data or integrate to generate time series data coming from a 3rd party system.

Fig 1- A very simplistic diagram of data exchange between different SAP systems

 

SAP APM currently has the following major components apart from the Asset Master Data replicated from S4-

  • Asset Health: This component enables Predictive Maintenance.
  • Asset Reliability Engineering: This component enables Reliability Centered Maintenance (RCM) and Failure Mode Effect Analysis (FMEA).
  • Asset Risk Assessment: This component enables identification of critical assets.
  • Asset Performance Analysis: This component consists of SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) which enables ease of creation of reports and dashboards and Failure Curve Analytics (FCA).
  • Industrial Systems: This component is the latest application in SAP APM and it can be used to define systems, which could be a combination of technical objects from different hierarchies.

 

I’ll try to elaborate a little on the different components I mentioned above.

  • Asset Health: With Asset Health you can define Rules on Indicators (measuring points replicated from S4 or defined locally to store calculations) of equipment to compare values and generate Alerts or Notifications if a certain condition is met (for ex. temperature above a threshold or differential pressure below a certain value). You can also view the trend of the time series data and predict failures based on the historical data for a group of equipment. You can also view and compare inspection data of the fixed assets and make decisions on their condition.

 

Fig 2- Indicator trend over time

 

  • Asset Reliability Engineering: You can identify critical equipment using Risk & Criticality and perform  Reliability Centered Maintenance (RCM) for a class of equipment under a certain operating context. Failure data can help you in defining Recommendations which could be sent to S4 as Maintenance Plans and Task Lists. You can also perform Failure Mode & Effect Analysis (FMEA).

 

Fig 3- An example of an RCM assessment

 

  • Asset Risk Assessment: You can identify critical equipment using Risk & Criticality. You can define templates which can then be used to perform risk and criticality assessments on the technical objects. This criticality can be updated in the ABC indicator of the technical objects.

 

Fig 4- An example of a R&C assessment

 

  • Asset Performance Analysis: Using SAC customers may create custom dashboards with various interactive reports, charts and tables can be generated to visualize the data. It may also be used for Failure Curve Analytics (FCA) to create groups of technical objects and then train models to predict the probability of failure for technical objects.

 

Fig 5- Failure curve of an equipment

 

  • Industrial Systems: This component enables definition and structuring of Industrial systems, which could be a combination of plant, functional locations and equipment from different hierarchies with different aspects such as function, product and location. This can give customers another way to define data which perform a function despite being in separate hierarchies in S4. An example could be firefighting system containing fire hydrants, water sprinkler, inert gas system, fire water pump, fire alarm and portable fire extinguishers. Another example could be fuel oil system of a ship which may contain bunker tanks, pipelines, purifiers, settling and service tanks, heaters, filters, fuel oil pumps, fuel nozzles, low fuel oil temperature and pressure alarms etc.

 

Fig 6- A generic example of an Industrial System in SAP APM

 

SAP APM is already being used or being implemented by some of the biggest O&G and Rail customers and customers in other industries. These partners are also contributing  heavily to various innovations using Customer Influence Requests (CIR).

I hope I was able to provide a very basic overview of SAP APM.

Stay tuned! In my upcoming blogs I’ll cover the benefits of SAP APM and why it should be implemented.

You may also read my other blogs-

SAP APM in Maritime Industry

Why do we need SAP Asset Performance Management (SAP APM)?

entry.title: What is SAP Asset & Service Management?
entry.content:
What is SAP Asset & Service Management?

SAP Asset & Service Management is a comprehensive, AI-powered suite for managing the entire asset lifecycle while driving operational excellence. This blog explores how it is setting new benchmarks in asset performance and reliability—and yes, there’s a demo video to showcase its impact.

Across asset-intensive industries, SAP Asset & Service Management delivers intelligent solutions to solve real-world challenges and unlock value. From oil, gas, & energy to utilities, travel/transportation, mining, manufacturing, aerospace & defense, and beyond, SAP is the trusted partner for organizations aiming to stay ahead.

Our solution combines advanced AI and automation with practical tools to optimize every aspect of asset operations—from strategic planning and predictive maintenance to efficient scheduling and seamless execution. With SAP, businesses don’t just manage assets; they drive operational excellence, improve sustainability, and continuously improve with closed feedback loops.

Watch this quick demo video to see SAP in action—empowering organizations to help their assets run better:

Reflecting high customer satisfaction in the enterprise asset management (EAM) market, IDC has awarded SAP the 2024 SaaS EAM Customer Satisfaction Award. This recognition underscores SAP’s dedication to delivering innovative solutions that empower organizations to achieve operational excellence and long-term sustainability.

Additionally, SAP has been recognized as a Leader in both the IDC MarketScape: Worldwide Field Service Management Applications 2023-2024 Vendor Assessment (doc #US49989523, December 2023) and IDC MarketScape: Worldwide Service Life-Cycle Management Platforms 2023-2024 Vendor Assessment (doc #US49989623, October 2023). Learn more about what analysts are saying about Asset & Service Management from SAP.

Key Strengths of SAP Asset & Service Management

Integral Part of SAP's ERP Suite

SAP Asset & Service Management is deeply embedded in the core ERP suite, offering advanced capabilities for Asset Performance Management and Field Service Management. This embedded approach ensures that complex asset and service requirements are met within a unified business environment.

SAP Asset & Service Management

AI-Infused Asset Lifecycle Management

SAP Business AI transforms the asset lifecycle, enabling predictive failure analysis, optimized scheduling, and actionable insights through tools like SAP’s Co-Pilot, Joule. These innovations simplify processes, enhance decision-making, and automate tasks, helping organizations proactively address challenges and adapt to changing demands.

AI-infused Asset & Service Management


A Unified Asset Data Thread

A digital thread of asset data ensures seamless integration of technical and operational information, creating a single source of truth for asset management. By unifying data across systems, this approach enhances governance, ensures accuracy, and enables organizations to make informed decisions while aligning with industry standards.

Integrate Asset & Service Management accross the enterprise

Closed-Loop Asset Management

A closed-loop asset management approach integrates asset data, risk assessments, maintenance strategies, and execution insights into a continuous improvement cycle. By leveraging IoT insights, advanced analytics, and automated workflows, it optimizes reliability, minimizes downtime, and enhances safety while aligning with ESG goals. The result is smarter, more sustainable asset performance with ongoing improvements.

Closed Loop Asset Lifecycle Management

Asset Reliability Engineering & Risk Management

Organizations can enhance reliability and manage risks at scale with a solution that identifies critical assets, prioritizes risk, and supports systematic maintenance strategies. This approach enables improved ESG outcomes and minimizes disruptions through smarter asset management.

Asset Reliability Engineering & Risk Management

Maintenance Planning and Optimization

Dynamic planning tools from SAP balance maintenance demands with available resources, making them ideal for Maintenance, Repair, and Overhaul (MRO) activities. Scenario-based simulations enable organizations to optimize schedules, reduce costs, and ensure timely maintenance while minimizing downtime.

Maintenance Planning and Optimization

EAM and FSM Convergence

SAP bridges the gap between Enterprise Asset Management (EAM) and Field Service Management (FSM), delivering a holistic solution for managing both internal and customer assets. This unified approach enhances operational efficiency and optimizes equipment performance across the board.

EAM and FSM Convergence

Sustainable and Safe Operations

Embedded sustainability and safety features ensure maintenance practices are compliant, safe, and aligned with regulatory goals. These capabilities span risk management, regulatory and permit compliance, and optimizing resource assignments through predictive routing to reduce travel times and CO₂ emissions. They also help prevent environmentally damaging asset failures while extending the useful life of assets and supporting long-term operational sustainability.

Sustainability and Compliance as Core Principles

Mobile Work Management

According to the US Bureau of Labor Statistics, job openings in installation, maintenance, and repair occupations are projected to grow significantly in the coming years. Coupled with a shortage of skilled labour, this makes mobile work management tools more critical than ever. SAP's mobile work management solutions enable field technicians to access work orders, asset and customer data, and instructions in real time—whether online or offline. With connected data and intuitive workflows, mobile workers are empowered to perform at their best.

Mobile Work Management

Comprehensive Health Monitoring and Analytics

With agile asset health monitoring and advanced analytics, SAP Asset Performance Management enables organizations to proactively manage asset health, uncover trends, and address issues before they escalate. By enhancing reliability, reducing unplanned downtime, and extending asset lifecycles, it delivers smarter, data-driven insights that optimize asset performance. 

Comprehensive Health Monitoring and Analytics

Collaborative Ecosystem and Business Networks

Collaboration is at the heart of effective asset management. An adaptable, extensible platform allows stakeholders to seamlessly share asset data and drive collaborative work management processes tailored to industry needs. Furthermore, partner solutions like Cumulocity IoT, Locana, and AsInt further enhance capabilities, enabling organizations to achieve mutual goals in asset performance and operational excellence.

SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration

The true measure of SAP Asset & Service Management’s impact is reflected in our customers' success. Discover how companies are revolutionizing their asset/service management practices and achieving operational excellence with SAP:

  • Equinor Boosts Asset Performance with Condition-Based Maintenance Enabled by SAP Asset Performance Management
  • Freeport-McMoRan: Using Mobile Apps to Make Asset Maintenance Faster, Safer, and More Efficient
  • Patterson Dental: How Does Integrated Field Service Management Help Save Time and Cut Costs?
  • Swiss Federal Railways Speeds Ahead with SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration

Organizations worldwide are unlocking the potential of SAP’s asset management solutions, improving reliability, boosting operational efficiency, and achieving sustainability goals—all with smarter, AI-powered tools designed for the future.

Ready to transform your asset and service management? Visit www.sap.com/eam to explore solutions, or register for our upcoming webinars for the latest insights.

Thoughts or questions? Feel free to share any feedback in a comment. For more information about SAP Asset & Service Management solutions, visit www.sap.com/eam. I also encourage you to register for our upcoming webinars for the most up to date information by registering here.

entry.title: Why do we need SAP Asset Performance Management (SAP APM)?
entry.content:
Everything evolves, and maintenance is no exception- continuously evolving into a more streamlined and efficient process!

With evolving technology, the maintenance practices have improved as well. With the advent of Industry 4.0 and availability of IoT there are new avenues for maintenance as well, which are cost effective and increases the reliability of machines. Data science, AI and ML can be used to predict and avoid failures based on historical data.

Maintenance always has budget constraints and I’m yet to see an industry where maintenance has unlimited resources to spend and keep the plant running without issues. Improved maintenance processes can save a lot of money and resources, while also increasing the reliability and predictability of the machines.

Historical Maintenance Process:

Historically, the only maintenance that we used to do was Reactive maintenance, which means you maintain something only when it breaks. This could be either corrective or breakdown in nature. The problem with this kind of maintenance is it is very costly and can cause long breakdowns where a line, a section or the whole plan is inoperative.

The reason Reactive maintenance is expensive has 3 major factors:

  1. Possible loss of production.
  2. Unplanned human resource requirements.
  3. Unplanned spare part requirements.

 

Current Maintenance Processes:

Apart from the historical Reactive maintenance, the maintenance practices slowly evolved into Proactive maintenance, which means doing repetitive maintenance tasks for instance changing certain spare parts of a machine such as gaskets, o-rings or changing the lubricating oil on a regular basis. The schedule or interval could be suggested by the OEMs or could be based on the experience of the operator. This was effective and helped avoid breakdowns. However, this was still expensive as the parts were being changed even when the parts are in good condition. This also might have environmental impact due to wastage of parts and oil. Proactive maintenance is still the most prevalent maintenance strategy in most industries.

The reason Proactive maintenance is expensive has 2 major factors:

  1. Excessive spare part consumption.
  2. Increased human resource requirements.

Benefits of Proactive maintenance:

  1. Increased reliability.
  2. Reduced unplanned shutdowns.

 

Future Maintenance Processes using SAP APM:

  • ­The next evolution in maintenance is Predictive and Prescriptive maintenance.

Predictive maintenance is a way to predict the future failures so that timely actions could be taken to plan and handle the failure. Prescriptive maintenance though on top of predicting the failure, also prescribes actions that can be taken to avoid that future failure.

For example, if the RPM of a purifier is lower than normal, then using SAP APM’s Asset Health module an Alert or a Notification could be generated to inspect the purifier.

  • Another evolution in maintenance is RCM- Reliability Centered Maintenance.

RCM dictates identifying critical assets/ machines, defining failure modes based on the operating context and creating a strategy for the maintenance of identified machines.

For example, we identify a fuel oil purifier as a critical piece of equipment since it supplies fuel oil to the main engine. We define operating context as heavy oil as the medium the purifier is processing. Then we define the maintenance strategies on it based on the experience and history of purifiers with the same operating context (fuel oil). Maintenance strategies can be defined in the form of reactive, proactive or improvement recommendations. The recommendations could be converted into time or counter based (for ex. run hours) maintenance plans and task lists. These strategies could be revisited based on the feedback and thus can be improved continuously.

This could also aid in predictive maintenance. For instance, while working on the notification created in the example under predictive/ prescriptive maintenance and converting it into a Work Order a task list could be included based on the Recommendations from SAP APM’s Asset Reliability Engineering module.

Benefits of Predictive maintenance:

  1. Increased reliability.
  2. Reduced maintenance cost.
  3. Consistently improving maintenance program.

 

So, to answer the original question which a lot of prospective customers’ would be asking, “why do we need SAP Asset Performance Management (APM)” -  SAP APM with it’s Asset Health and Asset Reliability Engineering modules can help industries transition from the traditional reactive & proactive maintenance to predictive, prescriptive maintenance and RCM. This will help in overall improvement of maintenance processes, increase in reliability and reduction in maintenance costs.

You can also read my other blogs:

What is SAP Asset Performance Management?

SAP APM in Maritime Industry

entry.title: What's New in SAP Asset Performance Management 2501 (including 2412)
entry.content:
Welcome to this highlights blog of our last two releases including:
  • Quickstart FMEA assessments – Reuse strategy assessments for classes to speed up FMEA creation.
  • Specify or find the origin of recommendations.
  • Long texts in alerts and notifications to enhance context and clarity.
  • Rule chaining – Execute rules sequentially to improve accuracy and avoid timing issues.
  • Direct navigation from alerts to technical objects to enable quick access to equipment details.
  • Public APIs for Alerts and Alert Types – Manage alerts and categories via the SAP Business Acceleration Hub.

Check out the videos below to see the features in action and don't forget to register here to join our 2502 webinar. If you register for an IAM webcast, you'll be automatically subscribed to our IAM Community Engagement Newsletter.

Let's dive in.

 

Asset Reliability Engineering

Quickstart FMEA Assessments Using Strategy Assessments for Classes

Creating an FMEA (Failure Modes and Effects Analysis) assessment from scratch can be time-consuming, but there's a smarter way to reuse existing strategy assessments to quickly build your failure data hierarchy.

You can quick start an FMEA assessment by copying failure data and recommendations from a Strategy Assessment for Class, filtered based on the class and failure data profile of the technical object.

  • Technical objects must share the same Class, Planning Plant, and Failure Data Profile

  • At least one released Strategy Assessment for Class exists for the selected class.

  • There are no duplicate failure modes between the source and FMEA assessment (to prevent copy failure).

Watch the video for more information and see the SAP Help Portal Quick Start of FMEA Assessments Using Strategy Assessments for Classes.

Specifying and Tracking Recommendation Origins

Being able to track or maintain recommendation origins enhances transparency and decision-making. Whether recommendations stem from legal/regulatory requirements, hazard operations, preventive maintenance reviews, quality standards or another source, specifying origins ensures better traceability and you can maintain multiple origins for each recommendation.

Origins are maintained in SAP S/4HANA. Once the recommendation is created, the object page opens in edit mode, where you can modify origins as long as the recommendation status is "In Process", "Created", or "Implementation in Process".

You can comfortably find recommendations from a specific source, such as quality standards using the filter and also add the Origin column to the list view where you can see all origins of all recommendations in one view.

Check out the video to see it in action.

 

Asset Health Monitoring

Enhancing Maintenance Decisions with Long Texts in Alerts

You can create and utilize "long texts" for alert types to provide clear, actionable context, enabling users to make faster, more informed decisions, thus improving operational efficiency and reducing resolution times. The feature includes:

  • Long Text Support: Alerts now include a long text field (up to 5000 characters) with rich text formatting, including clickable links, bullet points, and indentation.
  • Contextual Information: Provide essential details to guide troubleshooting and decision-making, such as actions, measures, or failure patterns.
  • Embedded URLs: Add links to additional resources or documentation.
  • API Enhancements:
    • Alert Type API: Read, create, and update long text for Alert Types.
    • Alert API: Read, create, and update long text for individual Alert instances.
  • Notification Integration: Pre-fill long text from Alerts into Notifications, with character count validation.

 Watch the video to find out more.

 

Rule Chaining for Sequential Execution*

Improve accuracy and reliability of rule execution by triggering rules based on the completion of other rules. This new feature eliminates timing issues and simplifies managing complex rule dependencies. We have introduced the following:

  • Chained Rules: Rules can now execute sequentially with one rule triggering the next.
  • Derivation & Aggregation: Chain both derivation and aggregation rules seamlessly.
  • Monitoring Alerts: Add a monitoring rule at the end of the chain to raise alerts.

In addition to schedule-based execution, triggering rules through a chain ensures reliable indicator values and avoids race conditions, making rule management efficient and dependable.

With this enhancement, you gain better control and consistency in sequential processes. Watch the video:

 

*only applicable for eIoT

 

New Navigation: Faster Access from Alerts to Technical Objects

We've introduced a new navigation feature that allows you to seamlessly access equipment details directly from alerts. The Technical Object in both the Alert List and Alert Details Page is now clickable, enabling direct navigation to the Explore Technical Objects app.

Benefits:

  • Faster Access to relevant equipment information.
  • Improved Efficiency by eliminating manual searches.
  • Streamlined Navigation for quicker handling and analysis of alerts.

This update simplifies your workflow, making it easier than ever to analyze alerts and access the details you need.

 

Public APIs for Alert Type and Alert

The Manage Alerts and Manage Alerts Types API are available now for public consumption on the SAP Business Acceleration Hub.

  • Manage Alerts: The API enables you to manage the alerts in SAP Asset Performance Management. Alerts help you with any potential failure that may occur or has already occurred for the technical object and helps to identify potential actions to be taken for the incoming alerts
  • Manage Alert Types: This API represents categories of master data objects to plan maintenance activities for technical objects. Create alert types with a unique ID, descriptions, and define Origin and Severity to support informed decisions and effective action planning.

For more information, see Alert and Alert Types Packages
